text: 100g of fat. The fat profile is doing most of the work here. For this category, the real question is fat quality.
0g of carbs. Everything else is kind of whatever. Not relevant here.
0g of protein. Everything else here is pretty flat. Also not relevant here.
Vitamin e at 93% daily value, vitamin k at 50% daily value stand out most. That gives this food real vitamin support.
Minerals are basically not adding much here.

Pros first: polyphenol reputation is a real plus. It brings a widely recognised antioxidant angle beyond the visible macro profile. Contains useful polyphenols. Adds value beyond the core fat chemistry alone. Very simple ingredient identity. A clear wholefood-adjacent advantage over more industrial fat products.

The drawbacks next: extremely energy dense. Still pure fat, so calories accumulate fast. Quality varies a lot. Source and authenticity matter in practice. Easy to overpour without noticing. A healthy image can make portion creep happen fast in practice.
S tier. Best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.
